mnemonics: NAND/NOR: 'Not AND' & 'Not OR' - Universal gates, can build ANY logic!
NOR is NOT OR, NAND is NOT AND - Remember the inversion!
keyPoints: NAND and NOR are 'universal gates'; any Boolean function can be implemented using only these gate types.
A NAND gate is equivalent to an AND gate followed by a NOT gate (inverted output).
A NOR gate is equivalent to an OR gate followed by a NOT gate (inverted output).
commonMistakes: Forgetting to apply De Morgan's Theorem when converting AND/OR logic to NAND-only or NOR-only implementations.
Incorrectly assuming the output is active-high (1) for a 'true' condition, when for these gates it is often active-low (0) in universal implementations.
